data.title: Bombardier's Global 7500 Jet sets 150th speed record, achieving speeds over 1,000 km/hr on 20 missions, breaking industry records.
data.description: In a press release today, Bombardier revealed that their Global 7500 business jet has achieved an impressive feat by setting its 150th speed record, surpassing all other business aircraft types. This high-performing jet has attained speed records for major city pairs like Tokyo to Los Angeles, Toronto to Paris, New York to London, and Hong Kong to Montreal. On 20 of these missions, the aircraft maintained an average speed exceeding 1,000 km/hr, ensuring swift travel for passengers. This remarkable achievement solidifies the Global 7500's reputation as a leader in the industry for speed and efficiency.

data.title: Costamare Inc. declares dividends on its preferred stocks: $0.476563 for Series B, $0.531250 for Series C, and $0.546875 for Series D.
data.description: Costamare Inc. (NYSE: CMRE) announced on October 2, 2025, that they will be paying cash dividends for their preferred stocks. The dividends declared are US $0.476563 per share for the 7.625% Series B Preferred Stock (NYSE: CMRE PR B), US $0.531250 per share for the 8.50% Series C Preferred Stock (NYSE: CMRE PR C), and US $0.546875 per share for the 8.75% Series D Preferred Stock (NYSE: CMRE PR D). These dividends cover the period from July 15, 2025, to October 14, 2025, and will be disbursed on October 15, 2025, to shareholders holding these preferred stocks as of the record date, which is October 14, 2025.

data.title: BeyondTrucks integrates AI into TMS platform, improving productivity and efficiency for enterprise fleets. Oct. 2, 2025.
data.description: In San Francisco on October 2, 2025, BeyondTrucks unveiled their transportation management system (TMS) aimed at boosting efficiency. This system is designed to replace outdated legacy software and manual processes for enterprise fleets. Integration will lead to enhanced productivity and operational efficiency in managing technology.

data.title: Weak ADP report shows US private payrolls dropped by 32,000 jobs in Sep vs expected gain of 51,000, driven by small/medium businesses. Fears of recession rise amid BLS shutdown.
data.description: An unexpected downturn in the private sector jobs market has sparked concerns of a looming economic recession, amid an ongoing government shutdown. The latest ADP Employment report revealed a surprising loss of 32,000 jobs in September, contrary to the anticipated gain of 51,000. The decline was concentrated in small and medium-sized businesses, painting a worrisome picture of the U.S. economy. With the Bureau of Labor Statistics withholding data due to the shutdown, the ADP report has gained significant influence, noted by Bill Adams, Chief Economist at Comerica Bank. Economist David Rosenberg, head of Rosenberg Research & Associates Inc., highlighted on X that recession chances are higher than the market assumes. The absence of BLS data has magnified the importance of private-sector reports like ADP. These developments signal a potential economic slowdown as noted by experts in the industry. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: BMC highlights latest innovations at ERS 2025 in Amsterdam, focusing on better patient care.
data.description: At the European Respiratory Society (ERS) International Congress 2025 in Amsterdam, BMC Medical, a renowned company specializing in sleep and respiratory solutions, presented its newest advancements. The event highlighted BMC's commitment to improving patient care through innovation. The congress attracted a large audience, with over 1,000 attendees participating in the showcase of BMC's latest products and technologies.

data.title: 7-Eleven's Laredo Taco Company offers BOGO lunch tacos and breakfast deals to celebrate National Taco Day on October 7.
data.description: Celebrate National Taco Day on October 7 with exciting offers from 7-Eleven, Inc.'s Laredo Taco Company® restaurants! Fans can enjoy BOGO lunch tacos and irresistible breakfast deals on this special day. Located in Irving, Texas, these restaurants are making it a taco lover's dream come true with their delicious offerings. Don't miss out on this opportunity to indulge in mouthwatering tacos and start your day off right with these crave-worthy deals.

data.title: Catherine Clay appointed as S&P Dow Jones Indices CEO starting Nov 1, 2025, replacing Dan Draper. Swamy Kocherlakota also to leave. Transition period for both executives.
data.description: On November 1, 2025, Catherine Clay will assume the role of CEO at S&P Dow Jones Indices, succeeding Dan Draper. Swamy Kocherlakota, the Chief Digital Solutions Officer, is also set to depart from S&P Global. Both Draper and Kocherlakota will stay on during a transition period. The news broke in New York in October.

data.title: Natural Grocers to donate 1% of sales on World Food Day (Oct. 16, 2025) to support The Cornucopia Institute, aiming to raise over $45,000.
data.description: A fundraising initiative is set to take place on October 16, 2025, with the goal of raising over $45,000 for a nonprofit organization based in LAKEWOOD, Colo., dedicated to safeguarding the integrity of organic practices. This campaign coincides with World Food Day. Natural Grocers®, a prominent organic and natural products retailer, will be leading this effort.

data.title: Dr. Bruce Beutler unlocks disease mystery, advises going outside; featured in Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Dr. Bruce Beutler, a noted scientist, suggests that to succeed in science, individuals should spend time outdoors. This advice has proven beneficial for Dr. Beutler, who uncovered crucial insights into disease mechanisms. His groundbreaking research has captivated readers, showcasing his remarkable contributions to the field. The post highlights Dr. Beutler's achievements, emphasizing his ongoing commitment to scientific exploration and innovation.

data.title: Tesla deliveries expected to rise with expiring EV tax credits boosting U.S. demand. TSLA stock increased, hitting highs in 2025.
data.description: Thursday marks the anticipated release of Tesla's third-quarter delivery numbers, with a surge in U.S. demand buoyed by expiring EV tax credits. This increase in demand has propelled Tesla's stock, which is currently near its peak at $2025. Investors are optimistic about the potential jump in Tesla deliveries due to the expiring tax credits.
